facts.text: Founded in 1869 by Alexander Turney Stewart, Garden City was one of America’s first planned communities, complete with wide avenues and a private railroad.
The Cathedral of the Incarnation, a striking Gothic Revival church, was erected by Stewart’s wife Cornelia in 1876 as a memorial to her husband.
The Waldorf School of Garden City, founded in 1947 as part of Adelphi University, was among the first Waldorf schools in the United States.
Long Island’s aviation heritage is celebrated at the Cradle of Aviation Museum in Garden City, showcasing early aircraft and flight pioneers.
Charles Lindbergh stayed at the Garden City Hotel before his historic 1927 flight, though the film The Spirit of St. Louis depicts a different scene.
